title: Fermented Juicy Comforting Lemon Stir-fry
description: This egg-free-friendly fermented and juicy Turkish dish features dry sherry for a unique flavor profile.

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free option)
1 cup fermented vegetables (such as pickles or kimchi, drained and rinsed)

instructions: Sterilize a glass jar by boiling it in hot water for 5 minutes.
Add the lemon zest and juice to the sterilized jar. Crush the lemon with your fingers to release more flavor.
Combine the dry sherry, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, honey/maple syrup, and soy sauce in a small bowl. Mix until smooth.
Pour the mixed ingredients into the jar with the zest and juice of the lemon. Seal the jar tightly and place it in a warm, dark spot for 24 hours. It should become bubbly.
After fermenting, remove the lid to release any pressure that might build up. Use tongs or a spoon to scrape away any froth on top.
Place a medium skillet over medium-high heat and add some oil. Once hot, swirl the pan to coat evenly.
Add the fermented vegetables into the pan and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until they start to wilt slightly.
Pour in the lemon mixture from the jar and let it cook down and reduce by half. The flavors will become intense and concentrated.
Serve hot with additional fermented vegetables or noodles as desired.
